title: senecio psammocecus
intro: Senecio psammocecus, also known as Desert Cotton or Groundsel Bush, is a perennial plant native to South Africa, characterized by its white, woolly stems and small, yellow daisy-like flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to act as a diuretic and to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. The health benefits of Senecio psammocecus include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential toxicity.
